# Security Policy

Thank you for helping keep this project secure. Please do not open public issues for security vulnerabilities — instead follow the reporting instructions below so we can address them responsibly.

## Supported Versions

- We recommend using the latest release of `exon-cli` and the templates included in this repository. Security fixes are applied to the latest release; older versions may not receive backported fixes.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

1. Send an email to: aurpitaurpit@gmail.com with the subject line `Security Report: exon`.
2. Include the following information in your report:
   - Component or package affected (for example: `templates/node-express-template-ts`)
   - Version(s) affected
   - A clear description of the vulnerability and the impact
   - Steps to reproduce or a minimal PoC
   - Any suggested mitigation or patches
   - Your contact information and whether we may contact you for follow-up

If you prefer encrypted communication, you may provide a PGP key in your initial message and we will respond with a key for encrypted details.

## How we handle reports

- We will acknowledge receipt within 3 business days.
- We aim to provide a remediation timeline and publish a fix within 90 days for non-critical issues; critical issues may be prioritised and fixed faster.
- After a fix is available, we will coordinate responsible disclosure and, if applicable, request a CVE assignment.

## After disclosure

Once a fix is released, we will update the relevant templates and packages and publish advisories as needed. Please wait for the coordinated disclosure timeline before publicly sharing exploit details.

## Other security topics

- For security questions that are not vulnerability reports, please open an issue or contact the maintainers at the email above.

Thank you for helping improve the security of this project.
